title: The Fifth Discipline
author: Peter M. Senge
subtopics: learning organizations, systems thinking, mental models
subline: The art and practice of the learning organization.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A feedback loop diagram — 5 disciplines of the learning organization arranged in a circle, with arrows showing their interactions. The 5th discipline (Systems Thinking) is in red. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1050)" fill="none">
    <!-- 5 circles arranged in a pentagon around a center -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5">
      <circle cx="0" cy="-360" r="60"/>
      <circle cx="342" cy="-110" r="60"/>
      <circle cx="212" cy="293" r="60"/>
      <circle cx="-212" cy="293" r="60"/>
      <circle cx="-342" cy="-110" r="60"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The 5th: a larger red circle in the center, SYSTEMS THINKING -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="120" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <text x="0" y="-6" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.04em">SYSTEMS</text>
    <text x="0" y="14" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.04em">THINKING</text>
    <!-- Connecting lines from each peripheral circle to the center -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1">
      <line x1="0" y1="-300" x2="0" y2="-120"/>
      <line x1="285" y1="-92" x2="106" y2="-43"/>
      <line x1="177" y1="245" x2="84" y2="106"/>
      <line x1="-177" y1="245" x2="-84" y2="106"/>
      <line x1="-285" y1="-92" x2="-106" y2="-43"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Labels for the 5 disciplines -->
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.04em" text-anchor="middle">
      <text x="0" y="-410">1 · PERSONAL MASTERY</text>
      <text x="370" y="-180">2 · MENTAL MODELS</text>
      <text x="240" y="390">3 · SHARED VISION</text>
      <text x="-240" y="390">4 · TEAM LEARNING</text>
      <text x="-370" y="-180">—</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Top label -->
    <text x="0" y="-520" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.18em">THE FIVE DISCIPLINES</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A feedback loop diagram of the 5 disciplines arranged in a pentagon. The 4 peripheral disciplines (Personal Mastery, Mental Models, Shared Vision, Team Learning) sit in small graphite circles around a central RED disc labeled 'SYSTEMS THINKING' — the only chromatic element, the 'fifth' discipline. 320px mixed-case 'The Fifth / Discipline' title sits below in weight 300. The visual thesis: systems thinking is the discipline that holds the others together.
